# UC-22.15.17 · OT Access from Untrusted Networks Without Split Tunnel Block

## Description

Flags VPN or agent sessions originating from high-risk geos or residential ASNs while accessing OT jump portals.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vpn and sourcetypes pan:globalprotect. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain country allowlist aligned to business; (2) require hardware token from untrusted regions; (3) integrate threat intel ASN feed; (4) auto-disable accounts on repeat hits. Indexes required: `index=vpn`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`; Lookups: `ot_jump_portals`, `ot_jump_portals.csv`, `ot_trusted_country`, `ot_trusted_country.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:globalprotect` with `public_ip`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain country allowlist aligned to business; (2) require hardware token from untrusted regions; (3) integrate threat intel ASN feed; (4) auto-disable accounts on repeat hits.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| iplocation public_ip
| lookup ot_trusted_country.csv Country OUTPUT trust
| where trust!="trusted" OR like(ASN,"*RESIDENTIAL*")
| lookup ot_jump_portals.csv portal OUTPUT sl_t
| stats earliest(_time) as first, latest(_time) as last, values(public_ip) as ips by user, portal, Country
| sort - last
```

Understanding this SPL

**OT Access from Untrusted Networks Without Split Tunnel Block (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.13)** — Flags VPN or agent sessions originating from high-risk geos or residential ASNs while accessing OT jump portals.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:globalprotect` with `public_ip`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: pan:globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="pan:globalprotect", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.

• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where trust!="trusted" OR like(ASN,"*RESIDENTIAL*")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `stats` aggregates events by **user, portal, Country**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
Output fields: Country, first, ips, last, portal, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Choropleth, Table, Single value (sessions).

## SPL

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| iplocation public_ip
| lookup ot_trusted_country.csv Country OUTPUT trust
| where trust!="trusted" OR like(ASN,"*RESIDENTIAL*")
| lookup ot_jump_portals.csv portal OUTPUT sl_t
| stats earliest(_time) as first, latest(_time) as last, values(public_ip) as ips by user, portal, Country
| sort - last
```

## Visualization

Choropleth, Table, Single value (sessions). Key fields: `Country`, `first`, `ips`, `last`, `portal`, `user`.
